meaning: adj.多虱卵的,<口>愚蠢的,笨的
examples: Time ran out before we could get down to the real nitty-gritty.
The scientific nitty-gritty appears in the journal Neuron this week.
We also only hinted at the nitty-gritty details of how stack overflows work.
We'll save the nitty-gritty for the proper launch of this 28nm chip later this year.
I'll end not with the nitty-gritty but with more laughs and with another duck.
